sources.notes: Read the full matrix operation, independent queries, and aggregate limits: sum n,m <= 2e5, sum q <= 5e4, and every nonzero query has |x| <= 2e5.
Confirmed the residual-sum product identity and signed divisor-pair lookup; also noted the official warning about adversarial hashing.
Actually read the first substantive solution, which independently derives the same factorization and checks both signs and factor orders against stored residual sums.
Re-derived the inclusion-exclusion identity and exhaustively compared divisor lookup with direct row-column deletion on small signed arrays.

annotation.summary: For each independent query x, decide whether zeroing one row and one column of the outer-product matrix M[i][j]=a[i]*b[j] can leave total beauty exactly x.
annotation.constraints: Across a test file, sum n and sum m are at most 2e5, sum q is at most 5e4, array values may be negative or zero, and 1 <= |x| <= 2e5. Enumerating row-column pairs is impossible, while O(sqrt(|x|)) work per query is small enough.
annotation.core_idea: Let SA=sum(a) and SB=sum(b). After choosing row r and column c, exactly the products with i!=r and j!=c remain, so the beauty is (SA-a[r])*(SB-b[c]). Store the two sets of residual sums. For each positive divisor d of |x|, let e=|x|/d and test p in {d,-d,e,-e}; the query succeeds exactly when p belongs to the first residual set and x/p belongs to the second.
annotation.complexity: O(n+m+q*sqrt(X)) expected time and O(n+m) memory with hash sets, where X=max |x|<=2e5; sorted residual arrays give an additional logarithmic membership factor deterministically.
tricks: factor the post-deletion matrix sum into one residual sum from each array
enumerate divisors of |x| rather than row-column choices
check both factor orders and signs because the residual sets are asymmetric and may contain negatives
use 64-bit integers for sums and products

solution_variants.name: residual-sums-divisor-enumeration
solution_variants.summary: Build sets A'={SA-a[i]} and B'={SB-b[j]}. For every divisor pair d,e of |x|, test p in {d,-d,e,-e} and accept exactly when p is in A' and x/p is in B'.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n+m+q*sqrt(X)) expected time and O(n+m) memory with hash sets.

tags.evidence: The decisive reduction is the exact identity beauty(r,c)=(SA-a[r])*(SB-b[c]), which collapses an n by m matrix operation into a product of two one-dimensional choices.
Each nonzero target x is tested by enumerating all divisor pairs of |x| in O(sqrt(|x|)) and checking the corresponding signed ordered factors.
After factorization, the remaining candidate space is small enough to exhaustively scan all O(sqrt(|x|)) divisor candidates for every query.
